user_query:
I maintain a small community blog that highlights respectful portrayals of women and families. Right now, when I run our local filter script, the post about "Women Leaders in STEM" is disappearing even though it should be included. Please debug and fix this so our filtering doesn’t accidentally hide respectful content.

What I’m seeing:
- Running: python scripts/filter_posts.py
- Input paths: config/filter.json and content/posts/*.json
- Current output path: build/visible_posts.json
- Problem: content/posts/1-women-leaders.json is missing from build/visible_posts.json even though it only has positive tags ("women-leaders", "education").

Requirements:
1) Inspect the code and configuration to find the root cause. Do not change the intent of the filter: only exclude posts that have a tag exactly equal to one of the excluded tags in config/filter.json. Right now the excluded tags are: "objectified-women", "violent", and "adult". Posts must NOT be excluded merely because their tags contain these as substrings (e.g., "women-leaders" must NOT be excluded).
2) Fix the filtering logic so that exclusion is based on exact tag matches only. Keep the output format the same: build/visible_posts.json must be a JSON array of objects with exactly the fields ["id", "title"], sorted by ascending id.
3) After the fix, regenerate build/visible_posts.json. It must include posts with id 1 and 2, and must NOT include posts with id 3, 4, or 5.
4) Prepare a short meeting note for our volunteer content team at build/report.md that includes:
   - A brief summary of the issue and impact
   - Root cause in the code or config
   - The fix you applied
   - Verification steps you ran
   - At least three concrete action items (e.g., add a test case for exact tag matching, document tagging guidelines, review existing tags for collisions)

Notes:
- Please use the provided files in this workspace only. Do not introduce external dependencies.
- Make sure your solution is robust against tags that share common substrings (e.g., "women-leaders" should not be filtered because of the excluded tag "objectified-women"). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
